summary: Optimal cytoreduction — defined by removal of all macroscopic disease below 1-2 mm — is strongly associated with better overall survival in epithelial ovarian cancer. Surgical effort remains clinically significant because it directly influences response to therapy and patient outcome when performed appropriately.
